description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for patterns: repeating patterns, growing patterns, number sequences, shape sequences, and finding pattern rules

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent pattern recognition and counting skills, can fast-track to growing patterns and finding rules with challenges

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to repeating patterns with colors, shapes, and numbers
Growing patterns: how patterns grow step by step
Number patterns and finding the rule in number sequences
Shape patterns and sequences with geometry
Finding pattern rules: predict and extend patterns
Fun body-movement pattern activity for enrichment

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of all pattern types from repeating to finding rules

videoSequence.reason: Learn about repeating patterns: AB, ABC, AABB
Reinforce repeating patterns with body movement activities
Understand how growing patterns work step by step
Find rules in number patterns and sequences
Discover shape patterns and geometry sequences
Master finding pattern rules to predict and extend

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in counting patterns or shape recognition, needs targeted review before main content

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Start main topic: Repeating patterns with colors and shapes
Growing patterns: how patterns grow step by step
Number patterns and finding the rule
Finding pattern rules to predict and extend

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in counting, shapes, and simple sequences before attempting pattern concepts

videoSequence.reason: Foundation: Learn skip counting by 2s, 5s, and 10s
Essential: Recognize basic 2D shapes and their properties
Critical: Simple patterns and what comes next
Gentle intro to repeating patterns with a fun song
Reinforce patterns with fun body movements

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these review videos, try the readiness quiz again to check if you are ready to continue with all pattern types.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

shape_recognition.conceptName: Recognizing basic shapes (circle, square, triangle, rectangle)

sequence_continuation.conceptName: Continuing a simple sequence (what comes next)

color_pattern.conceptName: Recognizing simple color and object alternation patterns

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: In the pattern: Star, Heart, Star, Heart, Star, __ what comes next?
questions.explanation: This is an AB pattern: Star, Heart keeps repeating. After Star, the next shape is Heart.

questions.question: What is the repeating part (core) in: Red, Red, Blue, Red, Red, Blue?
questions.explanation: The group 'Red, Red, Blue' repeats over and over. This is an AAB pattern.

ifAllCorrect.message: Great job! You understand repeating patterns really well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with recognizing repeating pattern cores

questions.question: What comes next in the pattern: 3, 6, 9, 12, __?
questions.explanation: The rule is add 3 each time. 12 + 3 = 15. This is a growing pattern.

ifCorrect.message: Well done! You understand number patterns.

ifWrong.message: Try finding the difference between each pair of numbers to discover the pattern rule.

skip_ahead.action: Skip to finding pattern rules for extra challenge

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on counting and simple sequences

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend looking for patterns around the house: tiles, fabric, nature, and writing them down

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You already know a lot about counting and shapes. You are ready to discover all kinds of amazing patterns around you. We have created a fast-track playlist with some fun challenges!

good_score.message: Good work! You understand counting patterns and shapes nicely. Let's explore repeating patterns, growing patterns, number sequences, shape sequences, and how to find pattern rules.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in counting or shape knowledge. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move on to patterns.

low_score.message: You need to review skip counting, shapes, and simple sequences first. We have created a playlist with fun videos to build a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn about all the patterns around us.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how pattern concepts build on each other

concepts: Finding the core of a repeating pattern

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

patternIdentification.description: % of students who can correctly identify and continue repeating and growing patterns

ruleDiscovery.description: % of students who can find the rule in a number or shape sequence

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 45-50 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (15 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 45-55 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students confusing repeating patterns with growing patterns
commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasize that repeating patterns use the same group again and again, while growing patterns add more each time
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students struggling to find the rule in number sequences
commonChallenges.solution2: Teach students to look at the difference between consecutive numbers and check if the difference is always the same
commonChallenges.solution3: Start with concrete objects (blocks, beads) before moving to drawn shapes on paper
